concept: Treasure / Value Highly / Regard as Precious
description: To regard something as precious and of great worth, holding it in high value. 'Themoka' names both the act of treasuring and what is treasured—the precious things we recognize as valuable, whether material, relational, or experiential.
sound_symbolism: The word holds value securely. 'The' /θe/ begins with gentle breath, recognizing what is precious. Then 'mo' embraces with nasal depth, value held close. Finally 'ka' confirms with velar certainty, worth acknowledged and protected.
grammatical_notes: Noun: 'mia themoka' (my treasure). Treasuring is cherishing: 'mia ha thena meropi.' (I cherish this thing.) The word names what is recognized as precious.

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: True treasures are not possessions but qualities and connections—wisdom, compassion, and relationship outvalue material wealth.
pillars.art-nouveau-aesthetics: Beauty itself is treasure—the precious moments, forms, and expressions that enrich existence.
